heading: Enforcement
label: 8314 Enforcement

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may gather and compile information and conduct any inspection or investigation that the Secretary considers to be necessary for the administration or enforcement of this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall have the power to subpoena the attendance and testimony of any witness, the production of all evidence (including books, papers, documents, electronically stored information, and other tangible things that constitute or contain evidence), or to require the person to whom the subpoena is directed to permit the inspection of premises relating to the administration or enforcement of this chapter or any matter under investigation in connection with this chapter.11See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Location of production
children.label: B Location of production
children.text: The attendance of any witness and production of evidence relevant to the inquiry may be required from any place in the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In case of disobedience to a subpoena by any person, the Secretary may request the Attorney General to invoke the aid of any court of the United States within the jurisdiction in which the investigation is conducted, or where the person resides, is found, transacts business, is licensed to do business, or is incorporated, to require the attendance and testimony of any witness, the production of evidence, or the inspection of premises.

children.heading: Noncompliance
children.label: ii Noncompliance
children.text: In case of a refusal to obey a subpoena issued to any person, a court may order the person to appear before the Secretary and give evidence concerning the matter in question, produce evidence, or permit the inspection of premises.

children.heading: Contempt
children.label: iii Contempt
children.text: Any failure to obey the order of the court may be punished by the court as contempt of the court.

children.heading: Witnesses
children.label: i Witnesses
children.text: A witness summoned by the Secretary under this chapter shall be paid the same fees and mileage that are paid to a witness in a court of the United States.

children.heading: Depositions
children.label: ii Depositions
children.text: A witness whose deposition is taken, and the person taking the deposition, shall be entitled to the same fees that are paid for similar services in a court of the United States.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: i Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall publish procedures for the issuance of subpoenas under this section.

children.heading: Review
children.label: ii Review
children.text: The procedures shall include a requirement that subpoenas be reviewed for legal sufficiency and, to be effective, be signed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Delegation
children.label: iii Delegation
children.text: If the authority to sign a subpoena is delegated to an agency other than the Office of Administrative Law Judges, the agency receiving the delegation shall seek review of the subpoena for legal sufficiency outside that agency.

prosecute, in the name of the United States, all criminal violations of this chapter that are referred to the Attorney General by the Secretary or are brought to the notice of the Attorney General by any person;

bring an action to enjoin the violation of or to compel compliance with this chapter, or to enjoin any interference by any person with the Secretary in carrying out this chapter, in any case in which the Secretary has reason to believe that the person has violated, or is about to violate this chapter or has interfered, or is about to interfere, with the actions of the Secretary; or
bring an action for the recovery of any unpaid civil penalty, funds under a reimbursable agreement, late payment penalty, or interest assessed under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The United States district courts, the District Court of Guam, the District Court of the Northern Mariana Islands, the District Court of the Virgin Islands, the highest court of American Samoa, and the United States courts of the other territories and possessions are vested with jurisdiction in all cases arising under this chapter.

children.heading: Venue
children.label: 2 Venue
children.text: Any action arising under this chapter may be brought, and process may be served, in the judicial district where a violation or interference occurred or is about to occur, or where the person charged with the violation, interference, impending violation, impending interference, or failure to pay resides, is found, transacts business, is licensed to do business, or is incorporated.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 3 Exception
children.text: Paragraphs (1) and (2) do not apply to sections 8309(c) and 8313(b) of this title.
